questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘ASK’ is written as ‘62’ and ‘BYE’ is written as ‘64’ How will ‘CRY’ be written in that language?
questions.explanation: Word 1“ASK” A = 1 S = 19 K = 11 Sum = 1 + 19 + 11 = 31 Double of 31 = 62 Word 2: "BYE" → 64 B = 2 Y = 25 E = 5 Sum = 2 + 25 + 5 = 32 Double of 32 = 64 Word: “CRY” C = 3 R = 18 Y = 25 Sum = 3 + 18 + 25 = 46 Code = 2 × 46 = 92

questions.explanation: @ and + are opposite faces. So, a can’t be formed. # and 6 are opposite faces. So, b can’t be formed. % and 4are opposite faces. So, c can’t be formed. questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number. 18 : 306 : : 23 : ? >उस विकल्प का चयन करें जो तीसरी संख्या से उसी प्रकार संबंधित है जिस प्रकार दूसरी संख्या पहली संख्या से संबंधित है। 18 : 306 : : 23 : ?

[NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: Logic: [1st number × 2] + second number = 3rd number. Now, For, [21, 34, 76] ⇒ [21 × 2] + 34 ⇒ 42 + 34 = 76. For, [39, 26, 104] ⇒ [39 × 2] + 26 ⇒ 78 + 26 = 104. Similarly, for Option 2: [28, 23, 79] ⇒ [28 × 2] + 23 ⇒ 56 + 23 = 79 Hence, the correct answer is "Option 2".

questions.explanation: 35×7-120÷12+38=273 Using BODMAS we will get 273 > 35×7-120÷12+38=273 BODMAS का उपयोग करने पर हमें 273 प्राप्त होगा

questions.question: Seven boxes, A, B, C, D, E, F and G, are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. G is kept immediately above E. F is kept immediately above D. Only A is kept above B. Only two boxes are kept above G. F is not kept at third position from the bottom. How many boxes are kept above F? >सात डिब्बे, A, B, C, D, E, F और G, एक के ऊपर एक रखे गए हैं, लेकिन जरूरी नहीं कि इसी क्रम में हों। G को E के ठीक ऊपर रखा गया है। F को D के ठीक ऊपर रखा गया है। केवल A को B के ऊपर रखा गया है। G के ऊपर केवल दो डिब्बे रखे गए हैं। F को नीचे से तीसरे स्थान पर नहीं रखा गया है। F के ऊपर कितने डिब्बे रखे गए हैं?
questions.explanation: Position Box 7 A 6 B 5 G 4 E 3 C 2 F 1 D > Position Box 7 A 6 B 5 G 4 E 3 C 2 F 1 D

questions.question: As per which article of the Indian Constitution, the Lok Sabha is the lower House of the parliament? > भारतीय संविधान के किस अनुच्छेद के अनुसार लोकसभा संसद का निचला सदन है?
questions.explanation: Article 79 of the Indian Constitution states that the Parliament of India consists of the President, the Rajya Sabha [Council of States], and the Lok Sabha [House of the People]. This article establishes the Lok Sabha as the lower house of Parliament. > भारतीय संविधान के अनुच्छेद 79 में कहा गया है कि भारत की संसद में राष्ट्रपति, राज्य सभा [राज्यों की परिषद] और लोक सभा [लोगों का सदन] शामिल हैं। यह अनुच्छेद लोक सभा को संसद के निचले सदन के रूप में स्थापित करता है।

questions.question: The Dhrang Fair is a two-day festival in __________________ and is celebrated in the honour of Saint Mekran Dada to pay homage and participate in religious rituals?
questions.explanation: The Dhrang Festival is a two-day festival celebrated in Gujarat in honor of Saint Makran Dada. Devotees visit his shrine, offer prayers, and participate in religious rituals. > ध्रांग महोत्सव गुजरात में संत मकरान दादा के सम्मान में मनाया जाने वाला दो दिवसीय उत्सव है। भक्तगण उनके मंदिर में जाते हैं, प्रार्थना करते हैं और धार्मिक अनुष्ठानों में भाग लेते हैं।

questions.question: Generally, what portion of the crop was collected as tax by the state during the Mahajanapada period?
questions.explanation: The most common rate of taxation was one-sixth [1/6] of the total produce, which farmers had to pay to the ruling authority. This tax helped in maintaining the administration, army, and public works. In some cases, additional taxes like Bali [voluntary offerings] and Kara [mandatory tax] were also collected. > कराधान की सबसे आम दर कुल उपज का छठा हिस्सा [1/6] थी, जिसे किसानों को शासकीय अधिकारी को देना पड़ता था। इस कर से प्रशासन, सेना और सार्वजनिक कार्यों को बनाए रखने में मदद मिलती थी। कुछ मामलों में, बाली [स्वैच्छिक प्रसाद] और कारा [अनिवार्य कर] जैसे अतिरिक्त कर भी वसूले जाते थे।

questions.question: How many times has the Ganga River been mentioned in the Rigveda? > ऋग्वेद में गंगा नदी का उल्लेख कितनी बार आया है?
questions.explanation: The Ganga River is mentioned only once in the Rig Veda [in Mandala 10, Hymn 75, Verse 5]. In contrast, the Saraswati and Indus [Sindhu] rivers are mentioned multiple times, indicating their greater significance during the Rigvedic period. > ऋग्वेद में गंगा नदी का उल्लेख केवल एक बार किया गया है [मंडल 10, भजन 75, श्लोक 5 में]। इसके विपरीत, सरस्वती और सिंधु नदियों का उल्लेख कई बार किया गया है, जो ऋग्वेदिक काल के दौरान उनके अधिक महत्व को दर्शाता है।

questions.question: In which type of climate is deciduous forests and tree-shredded grasslands found?
options: Tropical wet and dry climate>उष्ण कटिबंधीय आर्द्र और शुष्क जलवायु
Subtropical steppe climate>उपोष्ण कटिबंधीय मैदानी जलवायु
Humid subtropical climate >आर्द्र उपोष्ण कटिबंधीय जलवायु
Warm temperate [mid-latitude] climate>गर्म समशीतोष्ण [मध्य अक्षांश] जलवायु
questions.explanation: Deciduous forests and tree-shredded grasslands are typically found in regions with a Tropical Wet and Dry Climate [also called the Tropical Savanna Climate]. This climate has distinct wet and dry seasons, leading to the shedding of leaves by deciduous trees to conserve water during dry periods. Such climates are found in parts of India, Africa, South America, and Southeast Asia. > पर्णपाती वन और पेड़ों से कटे घास के मैदान आम तौर पर उष्णकटिबंधीय आर्द्र और शुष्क जलवायु वाले क्षेत्रों में पाए जाते हैं [जिसे उष्णकटिबंधीय सवाना जलवायु भी कहा जाता है]। इस जलवायु में अलग-अलग गीले और शुष्क मौसम होते हैं, जिसके कारण शुष्क अवधि के दौरान पानी को संरक्षित करने के लिए पर्णपाती पेड़ों द्वारा पत्तियों को गिरा दिया जाता है। ऐसी जलवायु भारत, अफ्रीका, दक्षिण अमेरिका और दक्षिण पूर्व एशिया के कुछ हिस्सों में पाई जाती है।

questions.question: Which amongst the following statements is correct about Mughal emperor Akbar? > मुगल सम्राट अकबर के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कथन सही है?
options: He was declared emperor in 1602.>उन्हें 1602 में सम्राट घोषित किया गया
He founded a new religion called ‘Din-e Ilahi’.>उन्होंने ‘दीन-ए-इलाही’ नामक एक नए धर्म की स्थापना की
He was highly educated and knew many languages. >वह उच्च शिक्षित थे और कई भाषाएँ जानते थे
He fought and won the third battle of Panipat against the Hindu King Hemu>उन्होंने हिंदू राजा हेमू के खिलाफ पानीपत की तीसरी लड़ाई लड़ी और जीत हासिल की
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced Din-i-Ilahi in 1582, a syncretic religious movement combining elements of various faiths. Akbar was declared emperor in 1556, not 1602. Akbar was illiterate but had great intellectual curiosity and promoted learning. The Third Battle of Panipat [1761] was fought between the Marathas and Ahmad Shah Abdali, long after Akbar's reign. > अकबर ने 1582 में दीन-ए-इलाही की शुरुआत की, जो विभिन्न धर्मों के तत्वों को मिलाकर एक समन्वित धार्मिक आंदोलन था। अकबर को 1602 में नहीं, बल्कि 1556 में सम्राट घोषित किया गया था। अकबर निरक्षर थे, लेकिन उनमें बौद्धिक जिज्ञासा बहुत थी और उन्होंने शिक्षा को बढ़ावा दिया। पानीपत का तीसरा युद्ध [1761] अकबर के शासनकाल के बहुत बाद मराठों और अहमद शाह अब्दाली के बीच लड़ा गया था।

questions.question: Langveer dance is the folk dance of which state?
questions.explanation: Langvir Nritya is a martial art-based folk dance of Uttarakhand, especially popular in the Garhwal region. It is performed by male dancers who climb a tall vertical pole and balance on their stomachs while executing acrobatic moves. Traditional drums [Dhol-Damau] accompany the performance, adding rhythm and energy. This dance reflects the bravery and agility of the people of Uttarakhand. > लंगवीर नृत्य उत्तराखंड का एक मार्शल आर्ट-आधारित लोक नृत्य है, जो गढ़वाल क्षेत्र में विशेष रूप से लोकप्रिय है। यह पुरुष नर्तकों द्वारा किया जाता है जो एक ऊंचे ऊर्ध्वाधर खंभे पर चढ़ते हैं और कलाबाजी करते हुए अपने पेट पर संतुलन बनाते हैं। पारंपरिक ढोल [ढोल-दमाऊ] प्रदर्शन के साथ ताल और ऊर्जा जोड़ते हैं। यह नृत्य उत्तराखंड के लोगों की बहादुरी और चपलता को दर्शाता है।

questions.question: Under which scheme will 500 new 'waste to wealth' plants be established, as per the 2023-24 Union Budget of the Government of India? > भारत सरकार के 2023-24 के केंद्रीय बजट के अनुसार, किस योजना के तहत 500 नए 'वेस्ट टू वेल्थ' संयंत्र स्थापित किए जाएंगे?
questions.explanation: Under the 2023-24 Budget, the Government of India announced the establishment of 500 new Waste-to-Wealth plants under the GOBARdhan Scheme. This includes 200 Compressed Biogas [CBG] plants and 300 community-based biogas plants to promote a circular economy. The scheme focuses on converting organic waste [such as cattle dung and agricultural waste] into bio-energy and organic fertilizers. > 2023-24 के बजट में भारत सरकार ने गोबरधन योजना के तहत 500 नए वेस्ट-टू-वेल्थ प्लांट लगाने की घोषणा की है। इसमें सर्कुलर इकॉनमी को बढ़ावा देने के लिए 200 कंप्रेस्ड बायोगैस [सीबीजी] प्लांट और 300 कम्युनिटी-बेस्ड बायोगैस प्लांट शामिल हैं। यह योजना जैविक कचरे [जैसे मवेशियों का गोबर और कृषि अपशिष्ट] को जैव-ऊर्जा और जैविक खाद में बदलने पर केंद्रित है।

questions.question: The Harry Messel Award was given to Bibhab Kumar Talukdar for his conservation leadership in October 2024 by the? > अक्टूबर 2024 में बिभब कुमार तालुकदार को उनके संरक्षण नेतृत्व के लिए हैरी मेसेल पुरस्कार किसके द्वारा दिया गया?
options: Biodiversity Observation Commission>जैव विविधता अवलोकन आयोग
Animal Survival Commission >पशु जीवन रक्षा आयोग
questions.explanation: The Harry Messel Award for Conservation Leadership was bestowed upon Bibhab Kumar Talukdar in October 2024 by the Species Survival Commission [SSC] of the International Union for Conservation of Nature [IUCN]. Species Survival Commission [SSC]: A specialized commission under the IUCN, focusing on the conservation of species worldwide. > अक्टूबर 2024 में प्रकृति के संरक्षण के लिए अंतर्राष्ट्रीय संघ [आईयूसीएन] के प्रजाति अस्तित्व आयोग [एसएससी] द्वारा संरक्षण नेतृत्व के लिए हैरी मेसेल पुरस्कार बिभब कुमार तालुकदार को प्रदान किया गया। प्रजाति अस्तित्व आयोग [एसएससी]: आईयूसीएन के तहत एक विशेष आयोग, जो दुनिया भर में प्रजातियों के संरक्षण पर ध्यान केंद्रित करता है।

questions.question: Identify the organism that shows bioluminescence?
questions.explanation: Ctenophores [commonly known as comb jellies] exhibit bioluminescence, meaning they produce and emit light. This ability helps them in predation, communication, and camouflage in deep-sea environments. > सीटेनोफोरस [जिन्हें आम तौर पर कॉम्ब जेली के नाम से जाना जाता है] बायोल्यूमिनेसेंस प्रदर्शित करते हैं, जिसका अर्थ है कि वे प्रकाश उत्पन्न करते हैं और उत्सर्जित करते हैं। यह क्षमता उन्हें गहरे समुद्र के वातावरण में शिकार, संचार और छलावरण में मदद करती है

questions.question: According to the Indian Constitution, which of the following Schedule deals with the separation of powers of the government? > भारतीय संविधान के अनुसार, निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी अनुसूची सरकार की शक्तियों के पृथक्करण से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Schedule 7 of the Indian Constitution deals with the separation of powers between the Union and State governments. It contains three lists: Union List [Subjects under the Central Government] State List [Subjects under the State Government] Concurrent List [Subjects shared by both Central and State Governments] > भारतीय संविधान की अनुसूची 7 संघ और राज्य सरकारों के बीच शक्तियों के पृथक्करण से संबंधित है। इसमें तीन सूचियाँ हैं: संघ सूची [केंद्र सरकार के अधीन विषय] राज्य सूची [राज्य सरकार के अधीन विषय] समवर्ती सूची [केंद्र और राज्य दोनों सरकारों द्वारा साझा किए जाने वाले विषय]

questions.question: Which instrument was used to measure economic growth during the planning era?
options: Per Capita Gross Domestic Product>प्रति व्यक्ति सकल घरेलू उत्पाद
questions.explanation: During India's Planning Era [1951–2017], economic growth was primarily measured using Gross Domestic Product [GDP]. The Five-Year Plans aimed to achieve specific GDP growth targets to assess the country's economic progress. For instance, the Eighth Five-Year Plan [1992–1997] targeted an average annual GDP growth rate of 5.6%, and the actual growth achieved was 6.8%. > भारत के नियोजन काल [1951-2017] के दौरान, आर्थिक विकास को मुख्य रूप से सकल घरेलू उत्पाद [जीडीपी] का उपयोग करके मापा जाता था। पंचवर्षीय योजनाओं का उद्देश्य देश की आर्थिक प्रगति का आकलन करने के लिए विशिष्ट जीडीपी वृद्धि लक्ष्य प्राप्त करना था। उदाहरण के लिए, आठवीं पंचवर्षीय योजना [1992-1997] ने 5.6% की औसत वार्षिक जीडीपी वृद्धि दर का लक्ष्य रखा, और वास्तविक वृद्धि 6.8% रही।

questions.question: According to the Census of India 2011, ‘Sikhs formed what percentage of the country’s population?
questions.explanation: According to the Census of India 2011, Sikhs accounted for 1.72% of India's total population. This percentage represents the Sikh community's share among India's major religious groups. The highest Sikh population is found in Punjab, followed by states like Haryana, Delhi, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh. > भारत की जनगणना 2011 के अनुसार, भारत की कुल जनसंख्या में सिखों की हिस्सेदारी 1.72% है। यह प्रतिशत भारत के प्रमुख धार्मिक समूहों में सिख समुदाय की हिस्सेदारी को दर्शाता है। सबसे ज़्यादा सिख आबादी पंजाब में पाई जाती है, उसके बाद हरियाणा, दिल्ली, राजस्थान और उत्तर प्रदेश जैसे राज्य हैं।

questions.question: In which year did women take part in Modern Olympics for the first time?
questions.explanation: Women first participated in the Modern Olympics in the year 1900 during the Paris Olympics. They competed in five sports: tennis, golf, sailing, croquet, and equestrian. Charlotte Cooper [Great Britain] became the first female Olympic champion in tennis. >महिलाओं ने पहली बार वर्ष 1900 में पेरिस ओलंपिक के दौरान आधुनिक ओलंपिक में भाग लिया था। उन्होंने पाँच खेलों में भाग लिया: टेनिस, गोल्फ़, नौकायन, क्रोकेट और घुड़सवारी। चार्लोट कूपर [ग्रेट ब्रिटेन] टेनिस में पहली महिला ओलंपिक चैंपियन बनीं।

questions.question: Which of the following countries will host the World Badminton Championship event 2026?
questions.explanation: India was initially scheduled to host the 2023 Sudirman Cup. However, due to scheduling changes, the Badminton World Federation [BWF] awarded the 2023 event to China, and India was subsequently granted the hosting rights for the 2026 World Championships. > भारत को शुरू में 2023 सुदीरमन कप की मेज़बानी करनी थी। लेकिन, शेड्यूल में बदलाव के कारण, बैडमिंटन वर्ल्ड फ़ेडरेशन [BWF] ने 2023 के इवेंट की मेज़बानी चीन को दे दी और उसके बाद भारत को 2026 विश्व चैंपियनशिप की मेज़बानी का अधिकार दे दिया गया।

questions.question: Which dwarf planet orbits the sun in 310 years at a distance between 38.5 to 53 AU?
questions.explanation: Makemake is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt. It orbits the Sun in approximately 310 years. Its orbit lies between 38.5 AU to 53 AU [Astronomical Units], which places it beyond Pluto in the Kuiper Belt. > माकेमेक कुइपर बेल्ट में एक बौना ग्रह है। यह लगभग 310 वर्षों में सूर्य की परिक्रमा करता है। इसकी कक्षा 38.5 AU से 53 AU [खगोलीय इकाइयों] के बीच है, जो इसे कुइपर बेल्ट में प्लूटो से आगे रखती है।

questions.question: Which of the following rivers does NOT originate from the peninsular plateau? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी नदी प्रायद्वीपीय पठार से नहीं निकलती है?
questions.explanation: Gandak River originates from Nepal in the Himalayan region, not from the Peninsular Plateau. Chambal, Mahanadi, and Son originate from the Peninsular Plateau of India. > गंडक नदी हिमालय क्षेत्र में नेपाल से निकलती है, प्रायद्वीपीय पठार से नहीं। चंबल, महानदी और सोन भारत के प्रायद्वीपीय पठार से निकलती हैं।

questions.question: How many delegates participated in the second session of Indian National Congress? >भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस के दूसरे अधिवेशन में कितने प्रतिनिधियों ने भाग लिया?
questions.explanation: The Second Session of the Indian National Congress was held in 1886 in Calcutta and was presided over by Dadabhai Naoroji. The number of delegates increased from 72 in the first session [1885, Bombay] to 434 in the second session, reflecting the growing popularity of the INC. This session aimed to bring together nationalist leaders from different regions and focused on issues like representation in government, civil rights, and economic policies. > भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस का दूसरा अधिवेशन 1886 में कलकत्ता में हुआ था और इसकी अध्यक्षता दादाभाई नौरोजी ने की थी। पहले सत्र [1885, बॉम्बे] में प्रतिनिधियों की संख्या 72 से बढ़कर दूसरे सत्र में 434 हो गई, जो INC की बढ़ती लोकप्रियता को दर्शाता है। इस सत्र का उद्देश्य विभिन्न क्षेत्रों के राष्ट्रवादी नेताओं को एक साथ लाना था और सरकार में प्रतिनिधित्व, नागरिक अधिकार और आर्थिक नीतियों जैसे मुद्दों पर ध्यान केंद्रित करना था।

questions.question: Select the INCORRECT statement? > गलत कथन का चयन करें?
options: Male germ-cells are produced by pollen grain. >नर जनन-कोशिकाएँ पराग कणों द्वारा निर्मित होती हैं।
Female gamete is present in the stigma.>मादा युग्मक वर्तिकाग्र में मौजूद होता है।
Fertilisation gives us zygote. >निषेचन से हमें युग्मनज प्राप्त होता है।
The fusion of male gamete and female gamete is known as fertilisation.>नरयुग्मक और मादायुग्मक के संलयन को निषेचन के रूप में जाना जाता है।
questions.explanation: Male germ cells [pollen grains] are produced in the anther [Correct]. The female gamete [egg cell] is located in the ovule inside the ovary, not in the stigma [Incorrect]. The stigma is the part of the pistil that receives pollen. Fertilization leads to the formation of a zygote [Correct]. Fusion of male and female gametes is called fertilization [Correct]. > नर जनन कोशिकाएँ [पराग कण] परागकोश में उत्पन्न होती हैं [सही]। मादा युग्मक [अंडाणु] अंडाशय के अंदर बीजांड में स्थित होता है, वर्तिकाग्र में नहीं [गलत]। वर्तिकाग्र स्त्रीकेसर का वह भाग है जो पराग ग्रहण करता है। निषेचन से युग्मनज का निर्माण होता है [सही]। नर और मादा युग्मकों के संलयन को निषेचन कहते हैं [सही]।

questions.question: In Fleming's left hand rule, which physical quantity is represented by the middle finger?
options: Direction of the magnetic field>चुंबकीय क्षेत्र की दिशा
Direction of electric current>विद्युत धारा की दिशा
Direction of the motion of conductor >कंडक्टर की गति की दिशा
questions.explanation: Fleming's Left-Hand Rule is used to determine the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field. According to the rule: Thumb → Direction of Force [Motion of Conductor] Forefinger → Direction of Magnetic Field Middle Finger → Direction of Electric Current > फ्लेमिंग के बाएं हाथ के नियम का उपयोग चुंबकीय क्षेत्र में धारा ले जाने वाले कंडक्टर पर बल की दिशा निर्धारित करने के लिए किया जाता है। नियम के अनुसार: अंगूठा → बल की दिशा [कंडक्टर की गति] तर्जनी → चुंबकीय क्षेत्र की दिशा मध्यमा → विद्युत धारा की दिशा

questions.question: For preparation of which of the following compounds is the chlor-alkali process used?
questions.explanation: The Chlor-Alkali Process [also called the Chloralkali Process] is an industrial method used for the electrolysis of sodium chloride [NaCl] solution to produce: Sodium Hydroxide [NaOH] Chlorine gas [Cl₂] Hydrogen gas [H₂] > क्लोर-क्षार प्रक्रिया [जिसे क्लोरअल्कली प्रक्रिया भी कहा जाता है] एक औद्योगिक विधि है जिसका उपयोग सोडियम क्लोराइड [NaCl] घोल के इलेक्ट्रोलिसिस के लिए किया जाता है जिससे निम्नलिखित उत्पन्न होते हैं: सोडियम हाइड्रोक्साइड [NaOH] क्लोरीन गैस [Cl₂] हाइड्रोजन गैस [H₂]

questions.question: A body is dropped from a height of 20 metres with zero initial momentum; calculate it’s mass if it hits the ground with momentum 40 kg. m/s. [Assume the acceleration due to gravity 'g' = 10 m/s²]?
questions.explanation: Given Data: Initial velocity (u) = 0 m/s Height (h) = 20 m Acceleration due to gravity (g) = 10 m/s² Final momentum (p) = 40 kg·m/s Using the equation of motion: v² = u² + 2gh Substituting the values: v² = 0 + 2(10)(20) v² = 400 v = √400 = 20 m/s : Use Momentum Formula Momentum is given by: p = m × v Substituting values: 40 = m × 20 m = 40 / 20 = 2 kg

questions.question: According to Koeppen's Scheme of classification of climate, which of the following characteristics is associated with 'Group E'? > कोपेन की जलवायु वर्गीकरण योजना के अनुसार, निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी विशेषता 'समूह ई' से संबंधित है?
options: The average temperature of the coldest month is –3°C or below >सबसे ठंडे महीने का औसत तापमान -3°C या उससे कम होता है
The average temperature of the coldest month is higher than –3°C but below 18°C>सबसे ठंडे महीने का औसत तापमान -3°C से अधिक लेकिन 18°C ​​से कम होता है
The average temperature of the coldest month is 18°C or higher >सबसे ठंडे महीने का औसत तापमान 18°C ​​या उससे अधिक होता है
The average temperature for all months is below 10°C>सभी महीनों का औसत तापमान 10°C से नीचे है
questions.explanation: The average temperature for all months is below 10°C According to Köppen's Climate Classification, Group E represents polar climates. Characteristics of Group E climates: All months have an average temperature below 10°C. This includes Tundra [ET] and Ice Cap [EF] climates. Found in regions like Antarctica, Greenland, and the Arctic Circle. > सभी महीनों का औसत तापमान 10°C से कम रहता है कोपेन के जलवायु वर्गीकरण के अनुसार, समूह E ध्रुवीय जलवायु का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है। समूह E जलवायु की विशेषताएँ: सभी महीनों का औसत तापमान 10°C से कम रहता है। इसमें टुंड्रा [ET] और आइस कैप [EF] जलवायु शामिल हैं। अंटार्कटिका, ग्रीनलैंड और आर्कटिक सर्कल जैसे क्षेत्रों में पाया जाता है।

questions.question: By what name is the central extension of the western coastal plains of India known? >भारत के पश्चिमी तटीय मैदानों के मध्य विस्तार को किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Western Coastal Plains of India are divided into three major sections: 1. Konkan Coast – Extends from Maharashtra to Goa. 2. Kannad Plains – Covers the central part of the Western Coastal Plains, primarily in Karnataka. 3. Malabar Coast – Found in Kerala and known for its lagoons and backwaters. Since the question asks about the central stretch of the Western Coastal Plains, the correct answer is Kannad Plains [located in Karnataka]. >भारत के पश्चिमी तटीय मैदानों को तीन प्रमुख भागों में विभाजित किया गया है: 1. कोंकण तट - महाराष्ट्र से गोवा तक फैला हुआ है। 2. कन्नड़ मैदान - मुख्य रूप से कर्नाटक में पश्चिमी तटीय मैदानों के मध्य भाग को कवर करता है। 3. मालाबार तट - केरल में पाया जाता है और अपने लैगून और बैकवाटर के लिए जाना जाता है। चूंकि प्रश्न पश्चिमी तटीय मैदानों के मध्य भाग के बारे में पूछता है, इसलिए सही उत्तर कन्नड़ मैदान [कर्नाटक में स्थित] है।

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct with respect to the Badminton game and match length?
options: A match consists of the best of five games of 20 points>एक मैच में 20 अंकों के सर्वश्रेष्ठ पांच गेम शामिल होते हैं
A match consists of the best of three games of 20 points>एक मैच में 20 अंकों के तीन सर्वश्रेष्ठ गेम शामिल होते हैं
A match consists of the best of four games of 21 points >एक मैच में 21 अंकों के चार सर्वश्रेष्ठ गेम शामिल होते हैं
A match consists of the best of three games of 21 points>एक मैच में 21 अंकों के तीन सर्वश्रेष्ठ गेम शामिल होते हैं
questions.explanation: "A match consists of the best of 3 games of 21 points." In badminton, a match is played in a best-of-three format, meaning a player or team must win 2 out of 3 games to win the match. Each game is played up to 21 points. A player/team must have at least a 2-point lead to win [e.g., 21-19]. If the score reaches 20-20, the game continues until one side gains a 2-point advantage [e.g., 22-20, 23-21]. > "एक मैच में 21 अंकों के 3 गेम में से सर्वश्रेष्ठ गेम शामिल होते हैं।" बैडमिंटन में, मैच बेस्ट-ऑफ-थ्री प्रारूप में खेला जाता है, जिसका अर्थ है कि मैच जीतने के लिए खिलाड़ी या टीम को 3 में से 2 गेम जीतने होंगे। प्रत्येक गेम 21 अंकों तक खेला जाता है। जीतने के लिए खिलाड़ी/टीम के पास कम से कम 2 अंकों की बढ़त होनी चाहिए [उदाहरण के लिए, 21-19]। यदि स्कोर 20-20 तक पहुँच जाता है, तो खेल तब तक जारी रहता है जब तक कि एक पक्ष 2 अंकों की बढ़त हासिल नहीं कर लेता [उदाहरण के लिए, 22-20, 23-21]।

questions.explanation: In case of Monu, effective % change = 2y – y – = y – Given, profit is same, so 2500×y% = 4500×[y – ]% = 4y y = % > मोनू के मामले में, प्रभावी % परिवर्तन = 2y – y – = y – दिया गया है, लाभ समान है, इसलिए 2500×y% = 4500×[y – ]% = 4y ⇒ y = %

questions.question: Let X be the least number which when divided by 15, 18, 20 & 27 the remainder in each case is 10 & X is a multiple of 31. What least number should be added to X to make it a perfect square? > मान लीजिए X सबसे छोटी संख्या है जिसे 15, 18, 20 और 27 से विभाजित करने पर प्रत्येक स्थिति में शेष 10 बचता है और X, 31 का गुणज है। X को पूर्ण वर्ग बनाने के लिए उसमें कौन सी छोटी से छोटी संख्या जोड़ी जानी चाहिए?
questions.explanation: LCM of 15,18,20 & 27 = 27×4×5 = 540 So, required number = [540x + 10] which is divisible by 31. Since 527x is always divisible by 31 and [13x + 10] will be divisible by 31 when x = 4. So, the required number = 540×4+10 = 2170 As nearest perfect square number is 2209 = 472. Hence, to make it perfect square, we will have to add 39. > 15,18,20 और 27 का LCM = 27×4×5 = 540 अतः, अपेक्षित संख्या = [540x + 10] जो 31 से विभाज्य है। चूँकि 527x हमेशा 31 से विभाज्य होता है और [13x + 10] x = 4 होने पर 31 से विभाज्य होगा। अतः, अपेक्षित संख्या = 540×4+10 = 2170 चूँकि निकटतम पूर्ण वर्ग संख्या 2209 = 472 है। अतः, इसे पूर्ण वर्ग बनाने के लिए, हमें 39 जोड़ना होगा।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: The committee comprises of Explanation: • The verb "comprise" means "to consist of" or "to be made up of". • Since "comprise" already includes the meaning of "of", adding "of" after it is incorrect and redundant. Examples: • The bouquet comprises roses, lilies, and tulips. • India comprises 28 states. Corrected Sentence: " The committee comprises five members from different departments."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. You cannot lose what you _______ had.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Never Explanation: The word "never" is the most appropriate choice here. It indicates the idea that something was not possessed at any point in time, emphasizing that it is impossible to lose something you never had. This fits perfectly with the structure of the sentence. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Never – कभी नहीं – Used to emphasize that something has not happened at any point in time. Example: "You cannot lose what you never had." [This means you can't lose something that was never in your possession.] • Once – एक बार – Indicates that something was had at least once in the past. Example: "You cannot lose what you once had." [This implies that you did have it at some point and can now lose it.] • Always – हमेशा – Suggests ongoing possession or continuous action. Example: "You cannot lose what you always had." [This would imply you have always had it and cannot lose it, which doesn’t align with the meaning of never having had it.] • Have – है – Refers to present possession, which changes the meaning of the sentence. Example: "You cannot lose what you have had." [This implies that you had it in the past and might still lose it, which contradicts the original meaning.] Final Correct Sentence: "You cannot lose what you never had."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. All the proposals review and the decisions will be communicated by the end of the month.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Are to be reviewed Explanation: • The original sentence "All the proposals review and the decisions will be communicated by the end of the month" has an issue with verb tense and form. • The correct phrase is "are to be reviewed," as it is the passive voice form of the verb, which is appropriate when talking about the proposals being evaluated. • "Are to be reviewed" expresses that the proposals are scheduled or planned to be reviewed. Final Correct Sentence: "All the proposals are to be reviewed and the decisions will be communicated by the end of the month."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: My brother-in-law’s car was loaded with several bagfuls of rice.
My brother’s-in-law car was loaded with several bagsful of rice.
My brother-in-law’s car was loaded with several bagsful of rice.
My brother’s-in-law car was loaded with several bagfuls of rice.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: My brother-in-law’s car was loaded with several bagfuls of rice. Explanation: • The sentence is grammatically correct in both possessive construction and plural form. • "My brother-in-law’s" – This is the correct way to show possession with a compound noun. The apostrophe and 's' are added at the end of the full compound noun. • "bagfuls" – This is the correct plural form. The suffix -ful stays intact and -s is added to form the plural. • The structure and word choice make the sentence clear and grammatically sound.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. She had to go through a lot of difficulties to achieve success.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: No improvement Explanation: The phrasal verb "go through" is the most appropriate choice in this sentence. It means to experience something difficult or unpleasant, which perfectly matches the idea of facing a lot of difficulties on the way to success. Here are the meanings and examples of all the phrasal verbs: • Go through – गुज़रना / अनुभव करना – To experience or endure something, especially difficulties or unpleasant situations. Example: "She went through a tough time after the accident." • Go down – नीचे जाना / घटना – Usually refers to a decrease, or physically moving down. Example: "The sun goes down at 6 PM in winter." • Go on – जारी रखना – To continue. Example: "The show must go on despite the problems." • Go over – दोहराना / सावधानी से देखना – To review or examine carefully. Example: "Let’s go over the plan one more time." Final Correct Sentence: "She had to go through a lot of difficulties to achieve success."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. They lived ____ bread and milk during the lockdown.
questions.explanation: Correct answer: On Explanation: • "live on" means to survive or sustain oneself using a particular food or resource. It is commonly used with types of food or substances that provide nutrition. • "On" shows the specific item or items that are being lived on or relied upon during a particular period. Examples: • They lived on rice and vegetables for a month during their trip. • During the expedition, the team lived on canned food and water. Corrected Sentence: They lived on bread and milk during the lockdown.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. A disciplined person uses time wisely, avoids distractions, and does not give up easily. B. Similarly, in professional life, discipline leads to better performance and respect from others. C. Students with discipline complete their homework on time, revise regularly, and perform better in exams. D. Without discipline, people often lose focus, waste time, and fail to achieve what they want.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: ACBD Explanation: A. A disciplined person uses time wisely, avoids distractions, and does not give up easily. ➡️ This is a good opening sentence — defines a disciplined person. C. Students with discipline complete their homework on time, revise regularly, and perform better in exams. ➡️ Supports the first sentence with a student-specific example. B. Similarly, in professional life, discipline leads to better performance and respect from others. ➡️ Adds another example in professional life, building on C. D. Without discipline, people often lose focus, waste time, and fail to achieve what they want. ➡️ Provides a contrast to the benefits discussed earlier — a strong conclusion. Final Paragraph: A disciplined person uses time wisely, avoids distractions, and does not give up easily. Students with discipline complete their homework on time, revise regularly, and perform better in exams. Similarly, in professional life, discipline leads to better performance and respect from others. Without discipline, people often lose focus, waste time, and fail to achieve what they want.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Ravi handed the traveller a bottle of water.
options: A bottle of water was handed to Ravi by the traveller.
A bottle of water was handed to the traveller by Ravi.
The traveller was being handed a bottle by Ravi.
The traveller had to handed a bottle by Ravi.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence "Ravi handed the traveller a bottle of water" is in active voice, where "Ravi" is the subject, "handed" is the verb, and "a bottle of water" is the object. • To change it into passive voice, the object becomes the subject of the sentence. • The verb "handed" [past tense] becomes "was handed" in passive form. • The correct structure is: [Object] + was/were + past participle + by + subject Final Passive Voice Sentence: A bottle of water was handed to the traveller by Ravi.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Rina said to her friend, "Are you attending the workshop tomorrow?"
options: Rina asked her friend if she was attending the workshop the next day.
Rina asked her friend was she attending the workshop tomorrow.
Rina said to her friend that was she attending the workshop the next day.
Rina asked her friend if she is attending the workshop the next day.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence is a yes/no question in direct speech: "Are you attending the workshop tomorrow?" • The reporting verb "said to" changes to "asked" in indirect speech. • The auxiliary verb "are" changes to "was", and the pronoun "you" changes to "she" to match "her friend". • The word "tomorrow" changes to "the next day" in indirect speech. • Structure used for indirect speech of yes/no questions: [Subject] + asked + if/whether + [subject] + [was/were] + [verb+ing] + [time expression {changed}] Final Sentence: Rina asked her friend if she was attending the workshop the next day.
